meta.disclaimer: Generated from public market data and news for research and education. Not financial advice; data may be delayed, incomplete, or wrong.

items.title: AgentBeats: Agentifying Agent Assessment for Openness, Standardization, and Reproducibility
items.abstract: The paper introduces Agentified Agent Assessment (AAA), a novel framework where evaluation is conducted by judge agents interacting with participants via standardized protocols (A2A and MCP). This approach unifies the assessment interface, decoupling evaluation logic from agent implementation. AgentBeats is the concrete realization of AAA, providing a generic, reproducible, and interoperable system for benchmarking diverse agent designs.
items.figure_caption: Figure 1 . Comparison between Traditional LLM/Agent benchmarks and AAA. AAA reduces the number of integrations from N × M N\( \times \) M to N + M N+M , while completely separating the benchmark and target agent as shown in the gray boxes.

items.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
items.abstract: Agents-K1 introduces an end-to-end pipeline to transform raw scientific documents into agent-native knowledge graphs, addressing the limitations of existing LLM agents in scientific knowledge orchestration. Its core method involves a multimodal parser capturing detailed entities, evidence, and relations across the full paper, supported by a specialized information-extraction backbone. The contribution is a richer, structured knowledge representation designed to facilitate complex scientific reasoning for AI agents.
items.figure_caption: Agents-K1 : Architecture and Capabilities. Left : Extracting multimodal knowledge from scientific papers. Middle : Schema-adaptive extensions for core research tasks. Right : Enhancing LLM reasoning and verifiable knowledge tracing.

items.title: ArogyaSutra: A Multi-Agent Framework for Multimodal Medical Reasoning in Indic Languages
items.abstract: ArogyaSutra is a multi-agent framework designed to enhance multimodal medical reasoning in Indic languages. It leverages a novel actor-critic architecture with dual-memory mechanisms and tool grounding to perform step-wise reasoning on complex medical queries involving text and images. The framework is supported by ArogyaBodha, a large-scale, multilingual multimodal medical Q\&A dataset spanning numerous body systems and imaging modalities.
items.figure_caption: Overview of the ArogyaSutra framework. ArogyaSutra employs an actor–critic architecture enhanced with tool-based image grounding and adaptive code-switching. The Actor first processes the input prompt and identifies the need for visual grounding, invoking appropriate tool agents to extract clinically relevant information from medical images before generating an answer with its associated reasoning. This output is then passed to the Critic for evaluation. If the response is correct, the Critic approves and outputs the final answer and reasoning. Otherwise, it consults an error detector (GPT-4o-mini) to identify the source of failure. Language-related errors trigger code-switching by translating the query into English, while reasoning-related errors are handled by incorporating summaries of past and current mistakes from long-term and short-term memory. The refined query is then fed back to the Actor for iterative refinement.

items.title: Can I Buy Your KV Cache?
items.abstract: This paper proposes a simple yet impactful method to eliminate redundant computation in large language models: **precomputing and selling the Key-Value (KV) cache for documents.** By allowing agents to buy and load a precomputed cache instead of re-running the expensive prefill step, the authors achieve significant compute savings (9-50x cheaper for a small model) with zero accuracy loss. The core contribution is demonstrating the feasibility and efficiency of treating the KV cache as a reusable, purchasable asset to drastically reduce inference costs.
items.figure_caption: Amortized per-call cost vs. reuse count N N (log–log). The from-scratch cost is flat at C prefill C_{\( \text{prefill} \)} ; KV-reuse falls as C prefill / N + C reuse C_{\( \text{prefill} \)}/N+C_{\( \text{reuse} \)} toward a floor of C reuse C_{\( \text{reuse} \)} .

items.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
items.abstract: The paper introduces **EurekAgent**, an agent system arguing that the bottleneck for autonomous scientific discovery is shifting to **agent environment engineering**. EurekAgent focuses on designing the environment—including resources, constraints, and interfaces—to amplify desired agent behaviors (like exploration and collaboration) and suppress negative ones (like reward hacking). This environment engineering approach is presented as the core method for achieving metric-driven, high-performance autonomous scientific discovery.
items.figure_caption: EurekAgent score evolution progress on the 26-circle packing problem.

items.title: Reasoning as Pattern Matching: Shared Mechanisms in Human and LLM Everyday Reasoning
items.abstract: This paper challenges the notion that human reasoning relies on abstract world models while LLMs only perform pattern matching. By testing both humans and LLMs on everyday common-sense reasoning, the authors found similar error patterns in both groups. They further demonstrated that specific LLM attention heads implement pattern-matching mechanisms that can predict seemingly irrelevant errors in human reasoning, suggesting both employ pattern-matching for everyday causal reasoning.
items.figure_caption: Overview of our evaluation probing human and LLM causal reasoning . a. Illustration of the evaluation format used for testing causal reasoning in people and LLMs. For each prompt, subjects are first presented with the scenario. After reading the prompt, subjects press SPACE to see the two response options and then select the most likely completion. b. Summary of the 11 categories we used in our evaluation of everyday causal reasoning.

items.title: Reward Modeling for Multi-Agent Orchestration
items.abstract: The paper introduces **Orchestration Reward Modeling (OrchRM)**, a self-supervised framework to evaluate the quality of multi-agent orchestration without requiring human labels. OrchRM constructs win-lose pairs from intermediate execution artifacts to train a Bradley-Terry reward model, enabling efficient, reward-guided orchestrator training and MAS scaling. This method significantly improves training efficiency (up to 10x token reduction) and enhances test-time scaling performance (up to 8% accuracy gain) compared to existing rollout-based methods.

items.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
items.abstract: EvoArena is a novel benchmark suite designed to evaluate LLM agents in dynamic environments by modeling progressive changes across terminal, software, and social domains. The core contribution is the introduction of EvoMem, a patch-based memory paradigm that explicitly tracks and structures memory evolution as update histories, allowing agents to reason about environmental changes. This framework reveals current agents' struggles in dynamic settings, while EvoMem demonstrates consistent performance improvements.
items.figure_caption: Step accuracy vs. chain accuracy on EvoArena. The closer to the upper-right corner the better.

items.title: HyperTool: Beyond Step-Wise Tool Calls for Tool-Augmented Agents
items.abstract: HyperTool addresses the execution-granularity mismatch in tool-augmented agents by introducing a unified, executable interface that allows models to invoke complex, multi-step tool workflows within a single outer call. This "folding" of deterministic subroutines reduces the number of model-visible decisions, saving context and simplifying low-level dataflow management. The method significantly improves performance on compositional tasks by enabling more abstract, higher-level reasoning about tool usage.
items.figure_caption: Comparison of context management paradigms in tool-augmented agents. (a) Step-wise execution expands atomic calls and observations into the trace. (b) Trace-level compression shortens the trace after expansion. (c) HyperTool manages context at execution time by folding dependent tool operations into one executable block and returning only the task-relevant result.

items.title: Recursive Agent Harnesses
items.abstract: The paper introduces the **Recursive Agent Harness (RAH)**, framing it as a code-first extension to model recursion, where the recursive unit is a full agent harness with tools and planning, not just a model call. RAH leverages a parent agent to generate and execute scripts that spawn parallel subagent harnesses for fine-grained tasks and use structured calls for smaller ones. This method significantly improves long-context reasoning performance, boosting a baseline coding agent from 71.75% to 81.36%.
items.figure_caption: Figure 1 . The Recursive Agent Harness (RAH). A parent agent selects between code-execution spawning (writing an executable script that spawns subagents in parallel) and JSON tool-call spawning (for 1–5 entries). Subagents carry the same spawning capability as their parent, enabling recursive decomposition bounded by a configurable depth limit.

items.title: A Three-Layer Framework for AI in Scientific Discovery
items.abstract: This paper introduces a **three-layer framework** for AI in scientific discovery, arguing that the crucial, yet underdeveloped, layer is **Layer 2: model formation through qualitative reasoning**. This layer involves recognizing the structural inadequacy of existing frameworks and understanding the problem within a broader representational space via structural insight, moving beyond mere search (Layer 1) or execution (Layer 3). The core contribution is emphasizing that true discovery requires this capacity for **structural insight and novel model creation**, not just optimization within existing paradigms.

items.title: Adaptive Turn-Taking for Real-time Multi-Party Voice Agents
items.abstract: This paper introduces **ModeratorLM**, a streaming speech large language model that adapts turn-taking behavior in multi-party conversations by conditioning it on an explicitly assigned conversational role. The core contribution is demonstrating that role-conditioning, especially enhanced with chain-of-thought reasoning, significantly improves turn-taking precision and recall (over 40% and 70% respectively) compared to non-role-conditioned baselines. This is validated using a novel synthetic dataset, RolePlayConv.
items.figure_caption: Example input–output sequence of the LLM for ``ModeratorLM-Think'' model. No reasoning trace is produced in Chunk 1. A reasoning trace appears in Chunk 2 without turn-taking, while in Chunk 3 the assistant takes the floor.

items.title: MiniMax Sparse Attention
items.abstract: MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) addresses the quadratic cost of long-context attention by integrating a lightweight Index Branch with Grouped Query Attention (GQA). This branch independently scores and selects a Top-k subset of key-value blocks for each GQA group, allowing the Main Branch to perform exact attention only over these relevant blocks. MSA's core contribution is providing a simple, scalable, and efficient block-sparse attention mechanism designed for practical speedups on GPUs in ultra-long-context scenarios.
items.figure_caption: Overview of MSA . The Index Branch (left) scores the full causal context with a single lightweight head and selects, for each query and GQA group, a set ℐ {\( \mathcal{I} \)} of k k key blocks; the local block is always included regardless of its score. The Main Branch (right) attends only to the selected blocks and produces the layer output. During training, a KL loss aligns the index distribution with the group-averaged Main Branch distribution on the selected blocks, and the Index Branch gradient is detached from the Main Branch.

items.title: Neuro-Symbolic Agents for Regulated Process Automation: Challenges and Research Agenda
items.abstract: This paper proposes **compliance-by-construction** as a core architectural paradigm for LLM agents operating in regulated industries, integrating existing symbolic structures (like regulations and process models) directly into the agent's decision-making framework. The core contribution is advocating for this structural foundation to proactively prevent control-flow violations, complementing traditional guardrail monitoring for semantic errors. The authors outline key neuro-symbolic research challenges necessary to achieve this integrated, compliant agent behavior.
items.figure_caption: Two-tier challenge architecture. The foundational tier (Challenge 1: regulatory operationalization; Challenge 2: symbolic process grounding with mediation interface) defines the structural base. The capability tier (Challenge 3: uncertainty-aware autonomy; Challenge 4: symbolic process memory; Challenge 5: cross-boundary explainability) extends it. Compliance-by-construction emerges as a property of addressing both tiers jointly by proactively preventing violations rather than detecting them post-hoc.

items.title: Toward Instructions-as-Code: Understanding the Impact of Instruction Files on Agentic Pull Requests
items.abstract: This paper investigates the impact of providing explicit instruction files on the performance of AI agents generating pull requests (Agentic-PRs). Analyzing 15,549 agentic PRs, the authors compare project performance (merge rate, complexity, merge time) before and after instruction file creation. The core finding is that specifying instructions for AI agents does not consistently lead to objectively better pull requests.
items.figure_caption: Figure 1 . The # of projects across merge rate improvement intervals for PRs closed before and after the first agent file creation.

items.title: Understanding the Rejection of Fixes Generated by Agentic Pull Requests -- Insights from the AIDev Dataset
items.abstract: This paper investigates why AI-generated code fixes in pull requests are frequently rejected, using a representative sample from the AIDev dataset. The core method involves a qualitative study followed by quantitative analysis to categorize the rejection reasons. The main contribution is the identification of 14 distinct failure modes, grouped into four high-level categories, providing crucial insights for improving the efficiency of AI coding agents.

items.title: Who Pays the Price? Stakeholder-Centric Prompt Injection Benchmarking for Real-world Web Agents
items.abstract: This paper introduces **StakeBench**, a novel benchmark for evaluating prompt injection attacks against web agents from a **stakeholder-centric** perspective. Unlike existing attack-centric methods, StakeBench systematically categorizes and attributes the resulting harm based on which specific stakeholder (e.g., user, website owner) is affected. This approach better reflects the real-world risk, where the impact and effectiveness of an attack are highly dependent on the targeted victim.
items.figure_caption: Overview of StakeBench . The agent operates within an interactive shopping interface where adversarial content embedded in environment surfaces such as reviews and ratings may steer execution away from the user’s benign intent. Three stakeholder categories define the harm space ( User , third-party Sellers , and the Platform ), spanning 12 attack objectives realized by 22 reusable templates (9 DPI, 13 IPI) and instantiated across 12 product categories to yield 264 executable adversarial cases. Each execution is labelled along three axes (ASR, TDR, BIR), with ASR and TDR jointly defining four failure regimes ranging from Robust Behavior to Compounded Failure.

items.title: Why Sampling Is Not Choosing: Intentionality, Agency, and Moral Responsibility in Large Language Models
items.abstract: This paper argues that Large Language Models (LLMs) do not possess the necessary agency for moral responsibility. The authors contend that genuine moral responsibility requires commitment-bearing agency grounded in *intrinsic* intentionality and self-attributed action, which LLMs lack. Their operation is purely probabilistic mapping, meaning their apparent intentionality is derived, and their outputs do not constitute genuine choices or commitments.

items.title: A2D2: Fine-Tuning Any-Length Discrete Diffusion for Adaptive Decoding
items.abstract: A2D2 introduces a unified framework for reward-guided fine-tuning of any-length discrete diffusion models by jointly optimizing insertion and unmasking policies. The core contribution is deriving the Radon-Nikodym derivative for the joint path measure, enabling theoretically guaranteed convergence to the reward-tilted distribution without needing target samples. This leads to the Adaptive Joint Decoding (AJD) loss, which minimizes decoding error by leveraging unmasking and insertion quality metrics.

items.title: Accelerating Speculative Diffusions via Block Verification
items.abstract: This paper introduces a novel method to efficiently adapt speculative decoding, traditionally used in LLMs, to continuous diffusion models by enabling block verification. This adaptation significantly improves the acceptance rate of draft predictions compared to existing diffusion acceleration techniques. The authors also formalize and analyze the "Free Drafter," a heuristic self-speculative mechanism for these diffusions.

items.title: AI agent bankrupted their operator while trying to scan DN42
items.summary: An AI agent attempting to scan the decentralized network DN42 inadvertently incurred massive costs, leading to the bankruptcy of its operator.

items.title: Claude Fable is relentlessly proactive
items.summary: The tech news story discusses the proactive nature of the AI model Claude Fable, suggesting it consistently takes initiative in its operations.

items.title: Workers are spending over 6 hours a week botsitting AI, fueling job frustration
items.summary: A recent report reveals that workers are spending over six hours weekly "botsitting" AI systems, leading to increased job frustration due to this hidden human labor.

items.title: Shall we play a game? My AI nuclear simulation
items.summary: A tech news story details the creation and implications of an AI-powered nuclear war simulation game developed by Kenneth Payne.

items.title: A jacket that harvests drinking water from the air
items.summary: Researchers have developed a jacket that can harvest potable drinking water directly from the surrounding air using a specialized material.

items.description: A tool for creating and running Linux containers using lightweight virtual machines on a Mac. It is written in Swift, and optimized for Apple silicon.
items.summary: Apple/container is a Swift-based tool for creating and running lightweight Linux containers on a Mac, specifically optimized for Apple silicon performance.

items.description: Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents.
items.summary: This repository provides production-grade engineering skills, encompassing tools and techniques, specifically designed to enhance the capabilities of AI coding agents.
OpenMed is an open-source healthcare AI platform providing tools and resources for developing and deploying artificial intelligence solutions in the medical field.

items.description: PM Skills Marketplace: 100+ agentic skills, commands, and plugins — from discovery to strategy, execution, launch, and growth.
items.summary: This repository, "pm-skills," functions as a marketplace offering over 100 agentic skills, commands, and plugins to support the entire product management lifecycle, from discovery and strategy through execution, launch, and growth.

items.description: Security scanner for AI agent skills. Detect vulnerabilities, malicious patterns, and security risks.
items.summary: SkillSpector is a security scanner designed to detect vulnerabilities, malicious patterns, and security risks within the skills of AI agents.

items.description: 🕵️‍♂️ Collect a dossier on a person by username from 3000+ sites
items.summary: Maigret is a tool that collects a comprehensive dossier on a person by querying over 3000 websites using a provided username.

items.description: FULL Augment Code, Claude Code, Cluely, CodeBuddy, Comet, Cursor, Devin AI, Junie, Kiro, Leap.new, Lovable, Manus, NotionAI, Orchids.app, Perplexity, Poke, Qoder, Replit, Same.dev, Trae, Traycer AI, VSCode Agent, Warp.dev, Windsurf, Xcode, Z.ai Code, Dia & v0. (And other Open Sourced) System Prompts, Internal Tools & AI Models
items.summary: This repository centralizes and shares system prompts, internal tools, and AI models for a wide array of AI coding assistants and development tools, including popular ones like Cursor, Devin AI, and NotionAI.

items.description: Desktop app to manage markdown knowledge bases
items.summary: Tolaria is a desktop application designed to efficiently manage and organize markdown-based knowledge bases.

items.description: An agentic skills framework & software development methodology that works.
items.summary: The **obra/superpowers** repository provides an agentic skills framework and software development methodology designed to be effective and functional.

arxiv.figure_caption: Step accuracy vs. chain accuracy on EvoArena. The closer to the upper-right corner the better.

arxiv.title: HyperTool: Beyond Step-Wise Tool Calls for Tool-Augmented Agents
arxiv.abstract: HyperTool addresses the execution-granularity mismatch in tool-augmented agents by introducing a unified, executable interface that allows models to invoke complex, multi-step tool workflows within a single outer call. This "folding" of deterministic subroutines reduces the number of model-visible decisions, saving context and simplifying low-level dataflow management. The method significantly improves performance on compositional tasks by enabling more abstract, higher-level reasoning about tool usage.
arxiv.figure_caption: Comparison of context management paradigms in tool-augmented agents. (a) Step-wise execution expands atomic calls and observations into the trace. (b) Trace-level compression shortens the trace after expansion. (c) HyperTool manages context at execution time by folding dependent tool operations into one executable block and returning only the task-relevant result.

arxiv.title: Recursive Agent Harnesses
arxiv.abstract: The paper introduces the **Recursive Agent Harness (RAH)**, framing it as a code-first extension to model recursion, where the recursive unit is a full agent harness with tools and planning, not just a model call. RAH leverages a parent agent to generate and execute scripts that spawn parallel subagent harnesses for fine-grained tasks and use structured calls for smaller ones. This method significantly improves long-context reasoning performance, boosting a baseline coding agent from 71.75% to 81.36%.
arxiv.figure_caption: Figure 1 . The Recursive Agent Harness (RAH). A parent agent selects between code-execution spawning (writing an executable script that spawns subagents in parallel) and JSON tool-call spawning (for 1–5 entries). Subagents carry the same spawning capability as their parent, enabling recursive decomposition bounded by a configurable depth limit.

arxiv.title: A Three-Layer Framework for AI in Scientific Discovery
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces a **three-layer framework** for AI in scientific discovery, arguing that the crucial, yet underdeveloped, layer is **Layer 2: model formation through qualitative reasoning**. This layer involves recognizing the structural inadequacy of existing frameworks and understanding the problem within a broader representational space via structural insight, moving beyond mere search (Layer 1) or execution (Layer 3). The core contribution is emphasizing that true discovery requires this capacity for **structural insight and novel model creation**, not just optimization within existing paradigms.

arxiv.title: Adaptive Turn-Taking for Real-time Multi-Party Voice Agents
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces **ModeratorLM**, a streaming speech large language model that adapts turn-taking behavior in multi-party conversations by conditioning it on an explicitly assigned conversational role. The core contribution is demonstrating that role-conditioning, especially enhanced with chain-of-thought reasoning, significantly improves turn-taking precision and recall (over 40% and 70% respectively) compared to non-role-conditioned baselines. This is validated using a novel synthetic dataset, RolePlayConv.
arxiv.figure_caption: Example input–output sequence of the LLM for ``ModeratorLM-Think'' model. No reasoning trace is produced in Chunk 1. A reasoning trace appears in Chunk 2 without turn-taking, while in Chunk 3 the assistant takes the floor.

arxiv.title: MiniMax Sparse Attention
arxiv.abstract: MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) addresses the quadratic cost of long-context attention by integrating a lightweight Index Branch with Grouped Query Attention (GQA). This branch independently scores and selects a Top-k subset of key-value blocks for each GQA group, allowing the Main Branch to perform exact attention only over these relevant blocks. MSA's core contribution is providing a simple, scalable, and efficient block-sparse attention mechanism designed for practical speedups on GPUs in ultra-long-context scenarios.
arxiv.figure_caption: Overview of MSA . The Index Branch (left) scores the full causal context with a single lightweight head and selects, for each query and GQA group, a set ℐ {\( \mathcal{I} \)} of k k key blocks; the local block is always included regardless of its score. The Main Branch (right) attends only to the selected blocks and produces the layer output. During training, a KL loss aligns the index distribution with the group-averaged Main Branch distribution on the selected blocks, and the Index Branch gradient is detached from the Main Branch.

arxiv.title: Neuro-Symbolic Agents for Regulated Process Automation: Challenges and Research Agenda
arxiv.abstract: This paper proposes **compliance-by-construction** as a core architectural paradigm for LLM agents operating in regulated industries, integrating existing symbolic structures (like regulations and process models) directly into the agent's decision-making framework. The core contribution is advocating for this structural foundation to proactively prevent control-flow violations, complementing traditional guardrail monitoring for semantic errors. The authors outline key neuro-symbolic research challenges necessary to achieve this integrated, compliant agent behavior.
arxiv.figure_caption: Two-tier challenge architecture. The foundational tier (Challenge 1: regulatory operationalization; Challenge 2: symbolic process grounding with mediation interface) defines the structural base. The capability tier (Challenge 3: uncertainty-aware autonomy; Challenge 4: symbolic process memory; Challenge 5: cross-boundary explainability) extends it. Compliance-by-construction emerges as a property of addressing both tiers jointly by proactively preventing violations rather than detecting them post-hoc.

arxiv.title: Toward Instructions-as-Code: Understanding the Impact of Instruction Files on Agentic Pull Requests
arxiv.abstract: This paper investigates the impact of providing explicit instruction files on the performance of AI agents generating pull requests (Agentic-PRs). Analyzing 15,549 agentic PRs, the authors compare project performance (merge rate, complexity, merge time) before and after instruction file creation. The core finding is that specifying instructions for AI agents does not consistently lead to objectively better pull requests.
arxiv.figure_caption: Figure 1 . The # of projects across merge rate improvement intervals for PRs closed before and after the first agent file creation.

arxiv.title: Understanding the Rejection of Fixes Generated by Agentic Pull Requests -- Insights from the AIDev Dataset
arxiv.abstract: This paper investigates why AI-generated code fixes in pull requests are frequently rejected, using a representative sample from the AIDev dataset. The core method involves a qualitative study followed by quantitative analysis to categorize the rejection reasons. The main contribution is the identification of 14 distinct failure modes, grouped into four high-level categories, providing crucial insights for improving the efficiency of AI coding agents.

arxiv.title: Who Pays the Price? Stakeholder-Centric Prompt Injection Benchmarking for Real-world Web Agents
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces **StakeBench**, a novel benchmark for evaluating prompt injection attacks against web agents from a **stakeholder-centric** perspective. Unlike existing attack-centric methods, StakeBench systematically categorizes and attributes the resulting harm based on which specific stakeholder (e.g., user, website owner) is affected. This approach better reflects the real-world risk, where the impact and effectiveness of an attack are highly dependent on the targeted victim.
arxiv.figure_caption: Overview of StakeBench . The agent operates within an interactive shopping interface where adversarial content embedded in environment surfaces such as reviews and ratings may steer execution away from the user’s benign intent. Three stakeholder categories define the harm space ( User , third-party Sellers , and the Platform ), spanning 12 attack objectives realized by 22 reusable templates (9 DPI, 13 IPI) and instantiated across 12 product categories to yield 264 executable adversarial cases. Each execution is labelled along three axes (ASR, TDR, BIR), with ASR and TDR jointly defining four failure regimes ranging from Robust Behavior to Compounded Failure.

arxiv.title: Why Sampling Is Not Choosing: Intentionality, Agency, and Moral Responsibility in Large Language Models
arxiv.abstract: This paper argues that Large Language Models (LLMs) do not possess the necessary agency for moral responsibility. The authors contend that genuine moral responsibility requires commitment-bearing agency grounded in *intrinsic* intentionality and self-attributed action, which LLMs lack. Their operation is purely probabilistic mapping, meaning their apparent intentionality is derived, and their outputs do not constitute genuine choices or commitments.

arxiv.title: A2D2: Fine-Tuning Any-Length Discrete Diffusion for Adaptive Decoding
arxiv.abstract: A2D2 introduces a unified framework for reward-guided fine-tuning of any-length discrete diffusion models by jointly optimizing insertion and unmasking policies. The core contribution is deriving the Radon-Nikodym derivative for the joint path measure, enabling theoretically guaranteed convergence to the reward-tilted distribution without needing target samples. This leads to the Adaptive Joint Decoding (AJD) loss, which minimizes decoding error by leveraging unmasking and insertion quality metrics.

arxiv.title: Accelerating Speculative Diffusions via Block Verification
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces a novel method to efficiently adapt speculative decoding, traditionally used in LLMs, to continuous diffusion models by enabling block verification. This adaptation significantly improves the acceptance rate of draft predictions compared to existing diffusion acceleration techniques. The authors also formalize and analyze the "Free Drafter," a heuristic self-speculative mechanism for these diffusions.

papers.title: AgentBeats: Agentifying Agent Assessment for Openness, Standardization, and Reproducibility
papers.abstract: The paper introduces Agentified Agent Assessment (AAA), a novel framework where evaluation is conducted by judge agents interacting with participants via standardized protocols (A2A and MCP). This approach unifies the assessment interface, decoupling evaluation logic from agent implementation. AgentBeats is the concrete realization of AAA, providing a generic, reproducible, and interoperable system for benchmarking diverse agent designs.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1 . Comparison between Traditional LLM/Agent benchmarks and AAA. AAA reduces the number of integrations from N × M N\( \times \) M to N + M N+M , while completely separating the benchmark and target agent as shown in the gray boxes.

papers.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
papers.abstract: Agents-K1 introduces an end-to-end pipeline to transform raw scientific documents into agent-native knowledge graphs, addressing the limitations of existing LLM agents in scientific knowledge orchestration. Its core method involves a multimodal parser capturing detailed entities, evidence, and relations across the full paper, supported by a specialized information-extraction backbone. The contribution is a richer, structured knowledge representation designed to facilitate complex scientific reasoning for AI agents.
papers.figure_caption: Agents-K1 : Architecture and Capabilities. Left : Extracting multimodal knowledge from scientific papers. Middle : Schema-adaptive extensions for core research tasks. Right : Enhancing LLM reasoning and verifiable knowledge tracing.

papers.title: ArogyaSutra: A Multi-Agent Framework for Multimodal Medical Reasoning in Indic Languages
papers.abstract: ArogyaSutra is a multi-agent framework designed to enhance multimodal medical reasoning in Indic languages. It leverages a novel actor-critic architecture with dual-memory mechanisms and tool grounding to perform step-wise reasoning on complex medical queries involving text and images. The framework is supported by ArogyaBodha, a large-scale, multilingual multimodal medical Q\&A dataset spanning numerous body systems and imaging modalities.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of the ArogyaSutra framework. ArogyaSutra employs an actor–critic architecture enhanced with tool-based image grounding and adaptive code-switching. The Actor first processes the input prompt and identifies the need for visual grounding, invoking appropriate tool agents to extract clinically relevant information from medical images before generating an answer with its associated reasoning. This output is then passed to the Critic for evaluation. If the response is correct, the Critic approves and outputs the final answer and reasoning. Otherwise, it consults an error detector (GPT-4o-mini) to identify the source of failure. Language-related errors trigger code-switching by translating the query into English, while reasoning-related errors are handled by incorporating summaries of past and current mistakes from long-term and short-term memory. The refined query is then fed back to the Actor for iterative refinement.

papers.title: Can I Buy Your KV Cache?
papers.abstract: This paper proposes a simple yet impactful method to eliminate redundant computation in large language models: **precomputing and selling the Key-Value (KV) cache for documents.** By allowing agents to buy and load a precomputed cache instead of re-running the expensive prefill step, the authors achieve significant compute savings (9-50x cheaper for a small model) with zero accuracy loss. The core contribution is demonstrating the feasibility and efficiency of treating the KV cache as a reusable, purchasable asset to drastically reduce inference costs.
papers.figure_caption: Amortized per-call cost vs. reuse count N N (log–log). The from-scratch cost is flat at C prefill C_{\( \text{prefill} \)} ; KV-reuse falls as C prefill / N + C reuse C_{\( \text{prefill} \)}/N+C_{\( \text{reuse} \)} toward a floor of C reuse C_{\( \text{reuse} \)} .

papers.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
papers.abstract: The paper introduces **EurekAgent**, an agent system arguing that the bottleneck for autonomous scientific discovery is shifting to **agent environment engineering**. EurekAgent focuses on designing the environment—including resources, constraints, and interfaces—to amplify desired agent behaviors (like exploration and collaboration) and suppress negative ones (like reward hacking). This environment engineering approach is presented as the core method for achieving metric-driven, high-performance autonomous scientific discovery.
papers.figure_caption: EurekAgent score evolution progress on the 26-circle packing problem.

papers.title: Reasoning as Pattern Matching: Shared Mechanisms in Human and LLM Everyday Reasoning
papers.abstract: This paper challenges the notion that human reasoning relies on abstract world models while LLMs only perform pattern matching. By testing both humans and LLMs on everyday common-sense reasoning, the authors found similar error patterns in both groups. They further demonstrated that specific LLM attention heads implement pattern-matching mechanisms that can predict seemingly irrelevant errors in human reasoning, suggesting both employ pattern-matching for everyday causal reasoning.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of our evaluation probing human and LLM causal reasoning . a. Illustration of the evaluation format used for testing causal reasoning in people and LLMs. For each prompt, subjects are first presented with the scenario. After reading the prompt, subjects press SPACE to see the two response options and then select the most likely completion. b. Summary of the 11 categories we used in our evaluation of everyday causal reasoning.

papers.title: Reward Modeling for Multi-Agent Orchestration
papers.abstract: The paper introduces **Orchestration Reward Modeling (OrchRM)**, a self-supervised framework to evaluate the quality of multi-agent orchestration without requiring human labels. OrchRM constructs win-lose pairs from intermediate execution artifacts to train a Bradley-Terry reward model, enabling efficient, reward-guided orchestrator training and MAS scaling. This method significantly improves training efficiency (up to 10x token reduction) and enhances test-time scaling performance (up to 8% accuracy gain) compared to existing rollout-based methods.

papers.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
papers.abstract: EvoArena is a novel benchmark suite designed to evaluate LLM agents in dynamic environments by modeling progressive changes across terminal, software, and social domains. The core contribution is the introduction of EvoMem, a patch-based memory paradigm that explicitly tracks and structures memory evolution as update histories, allowing agents to reason about environmental changes. This framework reveals current agents' struggles in dynamic settings, while EvoMem demonstrates consistent performance improvements.
papers.figure_caption: Step accuracy vs. chain accuracy on EvoArena. The closer to the upper-right corner the better.

papers.title: HyperTool: Beyond Step-Wise Tool Calls for Tool-Augmented Agents
papers.abstract: HyperTool addresses the execution-granularity mismatch in tool-augmented agents by introducing a unified, executable interface that allows models to invoke complex, multi-step tool workflows within a single outer call. This "folding" of deterministic subroutines reduces the number of model-visible decisions, saving context and simplifying low-level dataflow management. The method significantly improves performance on compositional tasks by enabling more abstract, higher-level reasoning about tool usage.
papers.figure_caption: Comparison of context management paradigms in tool-augmented agents. (a) Step-wise execution expands atomic calls and observations into the trace. (b) Trace-level compression shortens the trace after expansion. (c) HyperTool manages context at execution time by folding dependent tool operations into one executable block and returning only the task-relevant result.

RAH leverages a parent agent to generate and execute scripts that spawn parallel subagent harnesses for fine-grained tasks and use structured calls for smaller ones. This method significantly improves long-context reasoning performance, boosting a baseline coding agent from 71.75% to 81.36%.
papers.figure_caption: Figure 1 . The Recursive Agent Harness (RAH). A parent agent selects between code-execution spawning (writing an executable script that spawns subagents in parallel) and JSON tool-call spawning (for 1–5 entries). Subagents carry the same spawning capability as their parent, enabling recursive decomposition bounded by a configurable depth limit.

hacker_news.title: Claude Fable is relentlessly proactive

hacker_news.title: Workers are spending over 6 hours a week botsitting AI, fueling job frustration
hacker_news.summary: A recent report reveals that workers are spending over six hours weekly "botsitting" AI systems, leading to increased job frustration due to this hidden human labor.

hacker_news.title: Shall we play a game? My AI nuclear simulation
hacker_news.summary: A tech news story details the creation and implications of an AI-powered nuclear war simulation game developed by Kenneth Payne.

hacker_news.title: A jacket that harvests drinking water from the air
hacker_news.summary: Researchers have developed a jacket that can harvest potable drinking water directly from the surrounding air using a specialized material.

github_trending.description: A tool for creating and running Linux containers using lightweight virtual machines on a Mac. It is written in Swift, and optimized for Apple silicon.
github_trending.summary: Apple/container is a Swift-based tool for creating and running lightweight Linux containers on a Mac, specifically optimized for Apple silicon performance.

github_trending.description: Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents.
github_trending.summary: This repository provides production-grade engineering skills, encompassing tools and techniques, specifically designed to enhance the capabilities of AI coding agents.
OpenMed is an open-source healthcare AI platform providing tools and resources for developing and deploying artificial intelligence solutions in the medical field.

github_trending.description: PM Skills Marketplace: 100+ agentic skills, commands, and plugins — from discovery to strategy, execution, launch, and growth.
github_trending.summary: This repository, "pm-skills," functions as a marketplace offering over 100 agentic skills, commands, and plugins to support the entire product management lifecycle, from discovery and strategy through execution, launch, and growth.

github_trending.description: Security scanner for AI agent skills. Detect vulnerabilities, malicious patterns, and security risks.
github_trending.summary: SkillSpector is a security scanner designed to detect vulnerabilities, malicious patterns, and security risks within the skills of AI agents.

github_trending.description: 🕵️‍♂️ Collect a dossier on a person by username from 3000+ sites
github_trending.summary: Maigret is a tool that collects a comprehensive dossier on a person by querying over 3000 websites using a provided username.

github_trending.description: FULL Augment Code, Claude Code, Cluely, CodeBuddy, Comet, Cursor, Devin AI, Junie, Kiro, Leap.new, Lovable, Manus, NotionAI, Orchids.app, Perplexity, Poke, Qoder, Replit, Same.dev, Trae, Traycer AI, VSCode Agent, Warp.dev, Windsurf, Xcode, Z.ai Code, Dia & v0. (And other Open Sourced) System Prompts, Internal Tools & AI Models
github_trending.summary: This repository centralizes and shares system prompts, internal tools, and AI models for a wide array of AI coding assistants and development tools, including popular ones like Cursor, Devin AI, and NotionAI.

github_trending.description: Desktop app to manage markdown knowledge bases
github_trending.summary: Tolaria is a desktop application designed to efficiently manage and organize markdown-based knowledge bases.

github_trending.description: An agentic skills framework & software development methodology that works.
github_trending.summary: The **obra/superpowers** repository provides an agentic skills framework and software development methodology designed to be effective and functional.
